about.title: About Me
about.headline: Frontend Developer specializing in modern web interfaces
about.description: I build production-ready web applications using React, Next.js, and TypeScript. With experience across the full development lifecycle, I deliver clean, maintainable code and pixel-perfect implementations. My background transitioning from military service has instilled strong discipline, problem-solving skills, and the ability to thrive in fast-paced environments.

opsTracker.tagline: Production-grade Jira-like issue tracker — demonstrating scalable Next.js architecture with Supabase, RBAC, and real-world engineering patterns.
keyProps: Server Components + React Query hybrid data model
RBAC enforced at DB level via Supabase RLS
Handles large datasets with TanStack Virtual
opsTracker.description: Ops Tracker is a <highlight>full-stack</highlight> project management and issue tracking application demonstrating <highlight>production-level</highlight> frontend architecture backed by a real <highlight>Supabase</highlight> (PostgreSQL) database. It covers authentication, <highlight>role-based access control</highlight>, <highlight>Kanban boards</highlight>, project-scoped issues, <highlight>full audit trails</highlight>, <highlight>transactional email</highlight>, and <highlight>internationalisation</highlight> across three languages — everything a real <highlight>B2B SaaS</highlight> requires.

highlights.title: Next.js 16 App Router
highlights.description: Server Components, Server Actions, and proxy middleware drive both authentication and i18n end-to-end.

highlights.title: Row Level Security at the DB
highlights.description: Supabase RLS enforces every read and write at the database layer — zero-trust by default.

highlights.title: Three-Tier RBAC
highlights.description: User, Admin, and Super Admin roles are stored as a typed Postgres app_role enum and validated server-side.

highlights.title: Virtualised Issue Table
highlights.description: TanStack Table v8 paired with @tanstack/react-virtual keeps large issue lists smooth at any row count.

highlights.title: Playwright E2E Coverage
highlights.description: Critical authentication and issue workflows are guarded by end-to-end Playwright tests.

architectureSnapshot.title: Feature-based modules
architectureSnapshot.description: Each domain lives under src/features/<domain>/ with its components, hooks, actions, service, and Zod schemas colocated.

architectureSnapshot.title: Server-first data fetching
architectureSnapshot.description: Server Components handle the initial paint; TanStack Query takes over on the client for lists, filters, and optimistic mutations.

architectureSnapshot.title: Defense in depth
architectureSnapshot.description: Supabase RLS plus Server Action role checks enforce permissions twice — the client is never trusted for authorization.

architectureSnapshot.title: Append-only audit log
architectureSnapshot.description: Every mutating action is written to a PostgreSQL audit_logs table with typed action keys, actor, entity, and metadata.

defenseInDepth.label: RLS at the DB + Server Action checks

rlsAtDb.challenge: Client-side authorization is fragile — any leaked token or hand-crafted request can bypass UI guards.
rlsAtDb.solution: Authorization was pushed down into PostgreSQL via Supabase Row Level Security, with Server Actions performing a second role check before any mutation runs.
rlsAtDb.tradeoff: RLS policies are harder to debug than `if` statements and every table needs explicit coverage — but the system is zero-trust by default and the client is never trusted with permissions.

rscVsClient.challenge: Server Components give a fast, SEO-friendly first paint, but lists, filters, and inline editing need real client interactivity.
rscVsClient.solution: A hybrid model: Server Components prefetch data and dehydrate it into a HydrationBoundary, then TanStack Query takes over on the client for filtering, sorting, and optimistic updates.
rscVsClient.tradeoff: Two data layers raise the mental-model overhead — every feature has to decide where its data lives — but first paint stays fast and the UI never feels static.

auditLog.challenge: B2B SaaS needs full traceability: who did what, when, and to which entity — without coupling that bookkeeping to feature code.
auditLog.solution: An append-only PostgreSQL audit_logs table is written from a single, typed audit service. Action keys are a literal union, so every mutation has to declare its intent at the type level.
auditLog.tradeoff: Write amplification and storage growth are real, but the audit trail is the source of truth for compliance, debugging, and the in-app activity timelines.

monorepoFeatures.challenge: A flat src/components + src/hooks layout collapses under its own weight once a project has multiple bounded contexts.
monorepoFeatures.solution: Code is organised by feature (src/features/<domain>/) with components, hooks, server actions, services, and schemas colocated next to the domain they belong to.
monorepoFeatures.tradeoff: Cross-feature reuse takes a deliberate promotion step into shared/, but ownership is obvious and refactoring a single domain rarely touches anything outside its folder.

kanbanBoard.label: Drag-and-Drop Kanban Board
kanbanBoard.description: Per-project Kanban board powered by @dnd-kit with columns driven by configurable workflow statuses.

rbac.label: Role-Based Access Control
rbac.description: Three-tier RBAC — User, Admin, Super Admin — enforced at the database layer via Supabase RLS.

virtualTable.label: Virtualised Issue Table
virtualTable.description: Large issue lists rendered with TanStack Table v8 and @tanstack/react-virtual for smooth performance at scale.

auditTrail.label: Full Audit Trail
auditTrail.description: Every mutating action is recorded to PostgreSQL audit_logs with colour-coded action badges and meaningful summaries.

i18n.label: Internationalisation
i18n.description: Full i18n via next-intl across English, German, and Slovenian including all validation messages and error text.

adminPanel.label: Admin Control Panel
adminPanel.description: Dedicated /admin area for user role management, issue status CRUD, global audit log, and super-admin settings.

workExperience.title: Work Experience
workExperience.subtitle: Delivering production-ready frontend solutions across blockchain, transport and logistics for 6+ years.

highlights: Lead frontend development for blockchain-based products for nChain / Teranode (UK)
Build scalable web apps with React, Next.js, TypeScript and React Query
Mentor junior developers, conduct code reviews and drive frontend best practices
Contribute to architectural decisions for production-grade, high-traffic web apps
Built React applications for Slovenian Railways, Arriva, Nomago and Marprom
Developed ticket purchasing platforms and public transport passenger apps
Created interfaces for ticket vending machines deployed at train stations across Slovenia
Integrated backend APIs ensuring reliable data flow across complex transport systems
Developed web portals and admin interfaces for a smart parcel delivery SaaS platform
Built user, company and delivery-box management UIs serving an international client base
Integrated APIs and developed reusable UI components within a DotNetNuke CMS
Collaborated in an international agile team delivering cross-border logistics solutions
